prompt: Write a complete Python module named http_client.py that implements a production-quality HTTP client. The module must export: class HttpClient with __init__(self, base_url: str, timeout: float = 30.0, max_retries: int = 3, backoff_factor: float = 0.5), and methods get(path, params=None, headers=None), post(path, json=None, data=None, headers=None), put(path, json=None, headers=None), patch(path, json=None, headers=None), delete(path, headers=None), and close(). Each method returns an HttpResponse dataclass with fields status_code: int, headers: dict, body: bytes, json_data: dict | None, elapsed_ms: float, and method text() -> str. The client must implement: connection pooling using http.client.HTTPConnection reuse, automatic retry with exponential backoff on 5xx and connection errors, request/response logging at DEBUG level using the logging module, gzip decompression when Content-Encoding is gzip, JSON body serialization with Content-Type application/json header, session-level default headers, and a context manager interface (__enter__/__exit__). Also export: class HttpClientError(Exception) with attributes status_code and body; function build_url(base, path, params) -> str. Use only the standard library. No comments, no docstrings, code only.

Write a complete Python module named config_loader.py that implements a hierarchical configuration system. The module must export: class Config with __init__(self, data: dict, path: str = ''), methods get(key: str, default=None), require(key: str) -> Any (raises ConfigError if missing), section(key: str) -> Config (returns nested Config), set(key: str, value: Any), update(data: dict), to_dict() -> dict, and __getattr__ for attribute-style access; class ConfigLoader with methods load_file(path: str) -> Config, load_env(prefix: str = '', separator: str = '__') -> Config that reads environment variables, load_defaults(defaults: dict) -> Config, merge(*configs: Config) -> Config that deep-merges with later configs taking priority; class ConfigError(Exception); function load(path: str = None, env_prefix: str = None, defaults: dict = None) -> Config that is the main entry point and loads from all sources; type coercion: strings 'true'/'false' become bool, numeric strings become int or float, 'null'/'none' becomes None; support for .json, .yaml (if pyyaml available, else skip), and .toml (Python 3.11+ tomllib, else skip) file formats; ConfigValidator class with methods required(key: str), type_check(key: str, expected_type: type), range_check(key: str, min_val, max_val), and validate(config: Config) -> list[str] returning error messages. Use only the standard library plus optional pyyaml. No comments, no docstrings, code only.

Write a complete Python module named event_bus.py implementing a type-safe event bus with sync and async support. The module must export: dataclass Event with fields type: str, payload: Any, timestamp: float, source: str | None; class EventBus with methods subscribe(event_type: str, handler: Callable, priority: int = 0) -> str (returns subscription_id), subscribe_async(event_type: str, handler: Callable, priority: int = 0) -> str, unsubscribe(subscription_id: str) -> bool, publish(event: Event) -> int (returns handler count), publish_async(event: Event) -> Coroutine[int], publish_and_wait(event: Event, timeout: float = 5.0) -> list[Any] (collects return values), on(event_type: str, priority: int = 0) decorator for sync handlers, on_async(event_type: str, priority: int = 0) decorator for async handlers, once(event_type: str) decorator for one-shot handlers, middleware(fn: Callable[[Event], Event | None]) to add middleware that can transform or drop events, get_stats() -> dict with keys total_published, total_delivered, total_dropped, subscribers_by_type; class EventStore that wraps EventBus and stores all events in a deque with max_size, and has methods replay(event_type: str = None, since: float = None) and get_history(event_type: str = None, limit: int = 100) -> list[Event]; function create_bus(with_store: bool = False) -> EventBus | EventStore. Use only the standard library. No comments, no docstrings, code only.
